'''James Webster''' (born 11 July 1979) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] football coach who was the head coach of [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and a former professional player.

He was previously of the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. Webster played as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|hk}} in the [[National Rugby League]] for Australian clubs [[Balmain Tigers]] and [[Parramatta Eels]]. He then played in the [[Super League]] for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Hull F.C.]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]]. Webster stayed in England after retiring from playing and became a coach.

==Background==
Webster was born in [[Sydney]], New South Wales, Australia.

He is the older brother of former rugby league footballer and current [[New Zealand Warriors]] head coach, [[Andrew Webster (rugby league)|Andrew Webster]]

==Playing career==
===Balmain Tigers===
Webster played [[rugby league]] in his home town of [[Sydney]] for [[Balmain Tigers|Balmain]].  Webster played in Balmain's final ever match as a first grade side, a 42–14 loss against Canberra.  Balmain then went on to merge with fellow foundation club [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]] as part of the NRL rationalisation policy.

===Parramatta Eels===
Webster then moved to the [[Parramatta Eels]], making 22 appearances in his time with Parramatta.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/about/official-player-numbers/|title=Official Player Numbers|publisher=Parramatta Eels}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
Webster was released from [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on 2 May 2008.<ref name="Webster shown Hull KR exit door">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/7380265.stm|title=Webster shown Hull KR exit door|publisher=BBC|date=2008-05-02|access-date=2008-05-02}}</ref>

===Hull FC===
Webster eventually joined rivals Hull FC. After only one game in a black and white jersey – Webster was released from his contract.

===Widnes Vikings===
It was then announced he had signed a two-year contract with Widnes. He joined [[RFL Championship|National League One]] side [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] for the 2009 season.<ref name="Vikings swoop for Webster">{{cite web|url=http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby_league/s/1069310_vikings_swoop_for_webster|title=Vikings swoop for Webster|work=Manchester Evening News|date=2008-09-26|accessdate=2008-10-01}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
[[File:James Webster.jpg|thumb|right|Webster as Head Coach of Hull KR]]

===Hull FC===
Webster turned his back on his playing career to focus on his coaching role with former club Hull, when he returned to the black and whites as Assistant Coach in 2010. He worked closely alongside Head Coach [[Richard Agar]] and fellow Assistant Coach [[Andy Last]] in his first major coaching opportunity, whilst he is also involved in the development of other young coaches with the Rugby Football League. In early 2014, Webster was playing for amateur rugby club Hornsea RUFC, he earned one cap and also offered coaching support.

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
On 2 June 2014, Webster replaced the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] head coach Richard Agar as the new head coach of the [[Super League]] club.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30356/agar-replaced-by-webster | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140702073726/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30356/agar-replaced-by-webster | archive-date=2014-07-02| title=Agar replaced by Webster}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/9335474/super-league-richard-agar-steps-down-as-wakefield-coach|title = Super League: Richard Agar steps down as Wakefield coach}}</ref> On 19 May 2015, Webster left [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/may/19/wakefield-part-company-coach-james-webster-leigh-rugby-league|title = Wakefield part company with head coach James Webster after 11 months|website = [[The Guardian]]|date = 19 May 2015}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
In March 2016, Webster was named Interim Coach of [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] replacing the sacked [[Chris Chester (rugby league)|Chris Chester]]. Webster became the assistant coach at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] when he was replaced by [[Tim Sheens]] as head coach from the 2017 season.

===Featherstone Rovers===
In October 2019, was named head coach of [[Featherstone Rovers]] replacing Ryan Carr who return home to Australia. Webster guided Featherstone to the 2021 [[Million Pound Game]] where they lost against [[Toulouse Olympique]]. On 22 October 2021, he stood down from the role as head coach at Featherstone by mutual consent.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/james-webster-leaves-featherstone-as-club-give-him-parting-shot-after-honest-revelation-44520/|title=James Webster leaves Featherstone as club explains why after honest revelation|website=seriousaboutrl.com|date=22 October 2021}}</ref>
